channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASIO reaffirms focus on deadly attack prevention amid shifting resource allocations
item.description: ASIO has emphasised that preventing deadly attacks on Australians remains its top priority, despite reports indicating that a smaller proportion of national intelligence funding is being devoted to co...

item.title: Presidency defends Ramaphosa’s Zimbabwe visit amid DA criticism over timing and attendees
item.description: The South African Presidency has pushed back against criticism surrounding President Cyril Ramaphosa’s recent working visit to Zimbabwe, describing it as a routine diplomatic engagement rather than a ...

item.title: BINI expresses gratitude for Senate recognition, calling it a significant milestone
item.description: Filipino girl group BINI recently expressed deep gratitude after receiving recognition from the Philippine Senate. The acknowledgment highlights the group's achievements in the local and international...

item.title: Media Mogul Ted Turner, Founder of CNN, Passes Away at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the American media mogul and founder of CNN, has passed away at the age of 87. Turner was a revolutionary figure in television, establishing CNN in 1980 as the world's first 24-hour news c...

item.title: President Samia Appoints Dr Munisi as Deputy Minister, Emphasizes Unity and Professionalism
item.description: On 6th May 2026, President Samia Suluhu Hassan officially swore in Dr Eveline Munisi as Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Office responsible for Labour, Employment, and Industrial Relations. Dr ...

item.title: FG Sets Rules to Stop Abuse of Honorary Degrees in Nigerian Universities
item.description: The Federal Government don finally approve new rules to control how honorary degrees dey awarded for Nigerian universities. The Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, talk say plenty universities dey mi...

item.title: Minnesota Mayor Challenges Governor Over State Flag Redesign, Calls for Public Vote
item.description: Minnesota Governor Tim Walz is facing opposition from Champlin Mayor Ryan Sabas over the state's recent flag redesign. The new flag, approved by a 13-member commission in 2023, features a simplified d...

item.title: How Volunteers Organized a Massive Evacuation in Early War-Hit Kharkiv Region
item.description: At the start of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, parts of the Kharkiv region were cut off from the rest of the country, leaving residents trapped without official 'green corridors.' Local volunteer...

item.title: Moniepoint CEO clarifies shortage of senior tech talent in Nigeria and calls for skilled professionals
item.description: Moniepoint CEO, Tosin Eniolorunda, don clarify im statement wey cause gbege online. Earlier, he talk say the company get over 500 vacancies but dey find am hard to get Nigerians wey get the right seni...

item.title: Senate to Hold September Sittings in Kilifi to Enhance Public Participation
item.description: The Kenyan Senate has approved a plan to hold its plenary and committee sittings in Kilifi County for one week, from September 21 to 25, 2026. This initiative, commonly referred to as “Senate Mashinan...

item.title: De Lima responds to Padilla's remark on minority work in Congress
item.description: Senator Leila De Lima has publicly criticized Senator Robin Padilla for his recent comments regarding the role of the minority in Congress. De Lima expressed that Padilla’s statement indicates a misun...

item.title: Teenager jailed for possessing bomb-making videos and threatening a school attack
item.description: A teenage boy, identified as part of the 'incel' community, has been sentenced to prison after authorities discovered he had collected bomb-making videos and issued threats of a school shooting. Law e...

item.title: CEOs are divided over how AI will reshape white‑collar jobs and hiring
item.description: The Wall Street Journal piece highlights deepening disagreement among corporate leaders about how artificial intelligence will affect employment. While some CEOs openly warn that AI could eliminate a ...

item.title: US and Iran in talks over preliminary memorandum to end Gulf conflict
item.description: Iran and the United States are reportedly moving closer to agreeing on a one-page memorandum aimed at formally ending the ongoing conflict in the Gulf. According to sources, the preliminary document, ...

item.title: Share Your Favourite Canadian Summer Destinations
item.description: Summer travel looks different this year in Canada due to rising oil prices, higher airfares, and reduced flight availability. Many Canadians continue to avoid travelling to the U.S., while climbing ga...

item.title: Honda may indefinitely suspend its Ontario EV plant amid market and trade pressures
item.description: Honda, the Japanese automaker, is reportedly considering making the suspension of its $15-billion electric vehicle (EV) complex in Alliston, Ontario, indefinite, according to Japan’s Nikkei news agenc...

item.title: Alaska's Tracy Arm Fjord hit by second tallest tsunami ever recorded, study finds
item.description: A colossal tsunami struck Tracy Arm Fjord in Alaska on 10 August 2025, reaching an unprecedented height of 1,578 feet (481 metres), making it the second tallest ever recorded globally, after the 1958 ...

item.title: Asake Celebrates Wealth and Success in His Fourth Album ‘M$NEY’
item.description: Asake don release him fourth album wey him call ‘M$NEY’, and e dey clear say na him dey flex him wealth. The album na full celebration of success, luxury, and the good life, as e show how far Asake do...

item.title: Trump’s Private Ballroom Project May Result in $1 Billion Cost to US Taxpayers
item.description: A controversial ballroom project associated with former President Donald Trump, which was promoted as being privately funded, may ultimately cost U.S. taxpayers up to $1 billion. The project has faced...

item.title: Emerging singer Caleb Burke faces criticism for debut music amid brother's murder case
item.description: Caleb Burke, an emerging singer from the US, has faced public backlash after releasing music under the name Kova while his brother, D4vd, is in custody facing serious charges. The controversy erupted ...
